message:
Last winter, a blizzard rolled in faster than the forecast said. My sister texted the family group chat: 'Did everyone charge their phones?' My brother replied with a photo of his flashlight collection. My mom was asking if we had enough soup. 🍲

It hit me then—we weren't panicking. We were just... ready. Not in a 'doomsday bunker' way, but in that quiet way that comes from having thought about it beforehand.

Severe weather doesn't care about your timeline, but you can care about yours. A little advance thinking—charged devices, fresh water, knowing where your important stuff is—takes the chaos out of the actual day.

What's the one thing on your mind when a storm is heading your way?
